normalization.null_semantics: A null value means the current model's manufacturer did not publish a trustworthy revision-specific value in the reviewed sources.
normalization.source_policy: Technical specifications come from manufacturer product, support, news, or investor materials. A third-party source is used only for Sprout's reported market price and is labeled accordingly.
normalization.image_policy: Direct image URLs point to manufacturer-hosted press or product assets for research reference only. Copyright and reuse rights remain with their owners; storefront publication or model-training use requires a later rights check.

fictional_pricing_methodology.purpose: Provide coherent demo-store merchandising numbers where no public purchase price exists.
rules: Use an official current ownership price when one is publicly offered.
Keep subscription and deposit information separate from ownership price.
When only a reputable reported market price is available, label it reported rather than manufacturer-published.
For unavailable prices, assign a fictional showcase MSRP from market segment, size, payload, endurance, ruggedization, serviceability, development openness, and expected production volume.
Fictional values are UX placeholders, not quotes, valuations, forecasts, or claims about future manufacturer pricing.

attachment_match.basis: The 1X wordmark, soft full-body knit suit, minimal face, and human-scale proportions match current NEO product imagery.

robots.summary: A soft-bodied, tendon-driven biped intended for household chores, conversation, and progressively learned autonomy, with scheduled remote expert guidance for unfamiliar tasks.

watch_video.title: NEO is Starting to Learn on Its Own

market.availability: US deliveries stated to begin in 2026

specs.compute: 1X NEO Cortex using NVIDIA Jetson Thor; up to 2070 FP4 TFLOPS
notes: Total DoF is the sum of the manufacturer-published component counts.
Quick-charge time is published as six minutes per additional hour of runtime.

public_price.notes: Ownership and subscription are separate options; early-access terms may change.

visual_identity.head: Small oval head with a blank pale face and paired dark camera apertures.
visual_identity.torso: Human-proportioned soft torso with no exposed mechanical frame.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Long fabric-covered limbs and five-finger anthropomorphic hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Fully covered humanlike hips, legs, and flat soft feet.

blockers: Add exact side and rear views before final modeling.

open_issues: No official side or rear still was located.

attachment_match.basis: The Fourier logo, warm beige padded shell, narrow eye display, and GR-3 joint layout match Fourier's current care-bot.

robots.summary: A full-size social and assistive humanoid with a padded automotive-upholstery exterior, multimodal interaction, dexterous hands, developer interfaces, and hot-swappable batteries.

watch_video.title: Fourier GR-3: How to Be a Good Robot?

specs.compute: AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS, 32 GB memory, 1 TB storage, Ubuntu 22.04
sensing: distributed head and torso tactile sensors
notes: Fourier's brochure additionally describes RGB plus structured-light vision, four microphones, and 31 distributed pressure sensors.
Width and depth use the detailed Fourier support specification; announcement height rounds to 1.65 m.

store_price.basis: Full-size 55-DoF research and care platform priced above Sprout's reported developer price but below rugged industrial tiers.

visual_identity.head: Rounded rectangular cream head with a thin black eye visor and compact top sensor slot.
visual_identity.torso: Broad padded chest and abdomen with subtle Fourier mark.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Segmented beige padded arms with dark joint gaps and dexterous five-finger hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Mechanical biped legs softened by rounded padded armor panels.

blockers: Add a clean full-body front, exact side, and rear reference.

sources.title: Fourier Unveils Its First Care-Centric Humanoid GR-3

open_issues: Manufacturer pricing is private.
The official still set lacks full orthographic coverage.

attachment_match.basis: The Fauna Robotics label, compact white-and-yellow body, LED face, and exact limb proportions match Sprout Creator Edition.

robots.summary: A compact, lightweight, soft-bodied humanoid development platform built for safe interaction, modular autonomy, teleoperation, expressive behavior, and human-scale research environments.

watch_video.title: Meet Sprout | A Humanoid Robotics Platform for the Real World

specs.compute: NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin 64 GB with 1 TB SSD
notes: Published total DoF includes active eyebrows.
Swappable battery and grippers are included with Creator Edition.

public_price.notes: Associated Press reported the $50,000 price; Fauna's product page uses request-a-robot rather than public checkout.

store_price.basis: Reputable reporting, retained with explicit non-manufacturer label.

visual_identity.head: Small rounded head with wide black facial display, paired white LED eyes, and articulated eyebrows.
visual_identity.torso: Compact egg-shaped white torso with black waist separation.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Slim mechanical limbs with yellow shoulder caps and durable single-DoF grippers.
visual_identity.lower_body: Short five-DoF legs with rounded armor and wide low-impact feet.

blockers: Add a clean neutral front and exact side view.

open_issues: Price is reported rather than manufacturer-published.

attachment_match.basis: The Sunday mark, white wheeled pedestal, orange waist and shoulder accents, and suspended long arms match Memo.

robots.summary: A wheeled household robot with a telescoping body and long arms, designed to learn domestic manipulation from first-person data and perform chores autonomously without in-home teleoperation.

notes: Memo rests at about 4 ft and can reach about 7 ft.
Sunday says current hardware costs about $20,000 to build and expects manufacturing to reduce costs by at least 50%.
Published motion is software-limited to roughly half human pace; no absolute speed is given.

public_price.notes: Sunday publishes current build cost, not a retail offer.

store_price.basis: Rounded just below half of Sunday's stated approximately $20,000 current build cost, matching the company's stated expectation of at least a 50% manufacturing reduction.

visual_identity.head: Small white capsule head with a horizontal black eye strip and orange cap.
visual_identity.torso: Tall white-and-orange central body with a tapered waist and telescoping pedestal.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Extremely long slim white arms, black wrist modules, and compact dexterous hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Wide white circular mobile base with low-profile wheels.

blockers: Add clean full-body neutral front, side, rear, hand, and base views.

open_issues: No public mass, payload, runtime, DoF, or retail price.
Reference coverage is insufficient for accurate modeling.

attachment_match.basis: The Weave mark, sage fabric shell, small eye visor, telescoping column, dual arms, and wheeled base match Isaac 1.

robots.summary: A telescoping dual-arm home robot for laundry and daily room reset tasks, autonomous by default with teleoperation assistance when needed.

watch_video.title: Launching Isaac 1: our most capable home robot

market.form_factor: wheeled telescoping torso with two arms
market.availability: California deliveries fall 2026; broader US availability through 2027

sensing: head cameras with physical privacy shutters
notes: Total DoF is the sum of neck, arms, hands, torso, and base values published by Weave.

public_price.notes: Optional premium membership listed at $99 per month.

visual_identity.head: Small rounded rectangular head with dark visor and two bright eye apertures.
visual_identity.torso: Broad soft-covered shoulder body on an exposed telescoping center column.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Chunky rounded arm segments with compact multi-purpose grippers and orange tips.
visual_identity.lower_body: Single telescoping mast terminating in a broad rounded rectangular mobile base.

blockers: Add a clean rear view and isolated hand/base detail.

open_issues: Mass, payload, and rear design are not publicly documented.

robots.revision: electric Atlas, 2024 prototype to 2026 product lineage

attachment_match.basis: The Boston Dynamics mark and blue-and-black electric Atlas body are definitive; the attachment appears closer to the 2024-2025 prototype styling than the latest 2026 product render.

robots.summary: A rugged all-electric industrial humanoid for autonomous material handling and machine tending, engineered for continuous operation, high payloads, wide joint ranges, and industrial environmental protection.

market.availability: Boston Dynamics stated 2026 deployments were committed to Hyundai and Google DeepMind

notes: Payload categories are kept distinct because the manufacturer publishes instantaneous, sustained, and one-hand values.

store_price.basis: Top catalog tier based on 50 kg instantaneous payload, IP67 protection, wide temperature range, self-swapping battery, and scarce industrial deployment.

visual_identity.head: Compact cylindrical sensor head with a large circular dark face aperture.
visual_identity.torso: Dense rectangular blue core surrounded by black shoulder and waist mechanics.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Powerful black-and-blue segmented arms with industrial three-finger end effectors.
visual_identity.lower_body: Long reverse-articulated black legs with blue joint rings and broad industrial feet.

blockers: Choose attachment-era blue prototype or current 2026 production design before modeling; do not hybridize them.

open_issues: User approval must freeze whether the store represents the pictured prototype styling or current production styling.

attachment_match.basis: The Figure mark, white body, black face panel, three-dot chest mark, and current F.03 proportions match Figure 03.

robots.summary: A general-purpose electric humanoid using Figure's Helix AI for autonomous household and workforce tasks, with human-scale reach, dexterous hands, and a five-hour published runtime.

market.availability: Not offered through public retail checkout

specs.compute: Figure Helix embodied AI platform; detailed onboard compute not published
notes: Figure publishes a 2.3 kWh battery and up to 2 kW fast charging but not a full-charge time.

store_price.basis: Commercial full-size biped with 20 kg system payload and five-hour runtime, placed in the middle of the enterprise humanoid tier.

visual_identity.head: Featureless glossy black oval face embedded in a white head shell.
visual_identity.torso: Minimal white tapered chest with a vertical three-dot Figure mark.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Slim white arms, black elbows and wrists, and compact five-finger black hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: White thigh and shin armor over black mechanical joints, with narrow flat feet.

blockers: Add exact side, rear, and isolated hand views.

open_issues: No official price, DoF, side, or rear references.

robots.revision: current G1 and G1 EDU family
robots.summary: A compact, relatively low-cost humanoid platform for embodied AI research, education, and development, with a foldable body and optional higher-DoF dexterous hands in EDU configurations.

watch_video.title: Unitree Introducing | Unitree G1 Humanoid Agent | AI Avatar | Price from $16K

robots.selection_reason: Adds the most visible lower-price developer humanoid and a compact contrast to the full-size robots in the attachment.

market.availability: G1 and G1 EDU configurations sold by Unitree and distributors

specs.compute: 8-core high-performance CPU; optional NVIDIA Jetson Orin on EDU variants
notes: Unitree publishes a 9000 mAh quick-release battery but no battery voltage on the reviewed page, so it is not converted into kWh.
Secondary development is supported on EDU variants, not the base G1.

public_price.notes: Starting price excludes tax and shipping; higher-spec EDU configurations cost more.

visual_identity.head: Low-profile helmet-like sensor block without a human face.
visual_identity.torso: Compact dark mechanical chest with exposed waist articulation.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Slender segmented arms with simple grippers or optional dexterous hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Short powerful legs with prominent round joints and broad feet.

blockers: Use the base G1 configuration shown in the manufacturer reference set.

open_issues: A specific hand and compute configuration must be chosen for final art.

robots.summary: A commercial bipedal logistics robot designed to move totes and materials in human-oriented warehouses, with four-hour battery operation and deployment through Agility Arc fleet software.

watch_video.title: Digit's First Day of Work at GXO

robots.selection_reason: Represents the most mature warehouse-focused commercial humanoid segment and a distinctive digitigrade leg design.

notes: Height, mass, and payload are published by Agility in its GXO pilot materials.
Digit uses tote-oriented end effectors rather than anthropomorphic hands.

store_price.basis: Commercial logistics deployment, four-hour endurance, mature fleet integration, and 15.9 kg payload place Digit in the upper enterprise tier.

visual_identity.head: Rectangular white sensor head with two illuminated dark eye panels.
visual_identity.torso: Compact white-and-black torso with a rear battery pack and narrow waist.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Simple four-DoF arms terminating in broad tote-handling paddles.
visual_identity.lower_body: Signature backward-bending digitigrade legs with elongated lower links and narrow feet.

blockers: Add isolated end-effector and head close-ups.

open_issues: Agility does not publish a full current numeric specification table or price on the reviewed public pages.

robots.summary: A modular enterprise humanoid platform offered in bipedal and wheeled forms, designed for high-efficiency actuation, swappable energy systems, continuous operation, and Google DeepMind Gemini Robotics integration.

watch_video.title: Welcome to Robot Park, where Apptronik is building the future with Apollo 2

robots.selection_reason: Adds a leading US industrial humanoid with both bipedal and wheeled configurations and a current-generation modular design.

specs.compute: Google DeepMind Gemini Robotics integration announced; detailed onboard compute not published
notes: Apptronik publishes greater than 90% actuator efficiency and 7x22 operation goals.

Older first-generation Apollo figures such as 1.73 m height, 72.6 kg mass, 25 kg payload, and four-hour runtime are intentionally not copied into Apollo 2 fields without revision-specific confirmation.

store_price.basis: Current modular industrial platform with continuous-operation energy options, placed near other enterprise humanoids while acknowledging unpublished physical specs.

visual_identity.head: Rounded white head with a dark face visor and animated amber mouth line.
visual_identity.torso: Friendly white chest with a large expressive status display and tapered black waist.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Rounded modular arm shells with dark joints and compact dexterous hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Available with armored biped legs or a wheeled mobile base.

blockers: Add clean front, side, and rear product views.

open_issues: Apollo 2 revision-specific dimensions, mass, payload, runtime, and DoF are not public.
Locomotion configuration must be selected.

robots.summary: A wheeled general-purpose work robot optimized for high-quality manipulation data capture, improved sensing and telemetry, dexterous hands, and Carbon AI control.

watch_video.title: Sanctuary AI Unveils the Next Generation of AI Robotics

robots.selection_reason: Represents the dexterous teleoperation and AI-training branch of the market and provides a wheeled enterprise contrast to bipedal peers.

market.form_factor: humanoid upper body on wheeled base

notes: Sanctuary confirms a wheeled base for Gen 8.
The predecessor Phoenix platform used 20-DoF hands, but Gen 8 does not publish a complete current body spec sheet, so current hand DoF remains null.
Older Gen 6 dimensions, mass, payload, and speed are intentionally not copied into current Gen 8 scalar fields.

store_price.basis: Enterprise dexterous mobile-manipulation platform, discounted from bipedal peers for wheeled locomotion but priced for sophisticated hands and data capture.

visual_identity.head: Small dark sensor head with a bright eye or status region.
visual_identity.torso: Black industrial humanoid torso with exposed mechanical shoulders and a central column.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Anthropomorphic arms terminating in highly articulated five-finger dexterous hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Telescoping torso mounted to a stable wheeled base.

blockers: Official Gen 8 article assets are named Gen 7; obtain confirmed Gen 8-specific front, side, rear, and hand imagery before modeling.

sources.title: Sanctuary AI Releases New Generation of AI Robots

open_issues: Gen 8-specific public numeric specs are sparse.
Official article image filenames indicate the adjacent Gen 7 revision.

robots.summary: A mass-produced industrial humanoid with a dual-battery system and autonomous three-minute battery exchange for continuous 24/7 operation, plus a 52-DoF body and 15 kg full-range load handling.

watch_video.title: Meet UBTECH New-Gen of Industrial Humanoid Robot——Walker S2

robots.selection_reason: Adds a mass-produced industrial humanoid from China with a differentiated autonomous battery-swap system.

market.availability: Enterprise sales; UBTECH says mass production and delivery began in November 2025

specs.compute: UBTECH embodied AI and ROSA 2.0 platform; detailed onboard compute not published
notes: Published 24/7 operation depends on autonomous swapping between dual batteries and a swap station.
Waist rotation is published as plus or minus 162 degrees.
Unofficial height, mass, speed, and battery-duration figures are excluded.

capabilities: 15 kg material handling across 0 to 1.8 m

store_price.basis: Industrial 52-DoF platform with 15 kg full-range handling and autonomous 24/7 battery logistics, below Atlas because ruggedness and detailed payload limits are less fully documented.

visual_identity.head: Smooth white helmet form with a glossy black face visor and embedded camera array.
visual_identity.torso: Athletic white chest armor with black abdomen and narrow articulated waist.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Long white-and-black arms with compact multi-finger industrial hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Human-proportioned armored legs with black joint bands and broad white feet.

blockers: Add a neutral exact front and clean side view.
Obtain official height and mass before scale-critical modeling.

open_issues: Official public height, mass, speed, and per-charge duration were not located.

robots.summary: A cognitive humanoid family for industrial and domestic work, available in bipedal, wheeled, and extended-reach configurations with exchangeable forearms and multimodal interaction.

watch_video.title: Discover 4NE-1: The era of household humanoids is coming soon.

robots.selection_reason: Adds a European cognitive-robotics platform spanning industrial and home configurations.

specs.compute: NEURA cognitive platform with language model, computer vision, and reinforcement learning
notes: The current product page displays payload as 10-100 kg across different models; it is preserved as a family range rather than a single robot claim.
Current speed of 5 km/h is normalized to 1.389 m/s.
Prior-generation values are not mixed into Gen 3 fields.

store_price.basis: Commercial cognitive humanoid family with modular locomotion and tooling, placed mid-enterprise because configuration-specific payload and endurance are not public.

visual_identity.head: Small white-and-black helmet head with a horizontal dark sensor band.
visual_identity.torso: Broad angular white chest with black core and red branding accents.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Modular white arms with exchangeable forearms and anthropomorphic hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Configuration-dependent biped legs, wheeled base, or extended vertical axis.

blockers: Add exact front, side, rear, hand, and lower-body references for that configuration.

sources.title: Humanoid Robot 4NE1 for Work and Life

open_issues: Current page combines multiple models, so configuration-specific payload and imagery remain unresolved.

robots.summary: Tesla's general-purpose autonomous bipedal robot program, aimed at repetitive, unsafe, or boring tasks and trained with the company's AI, vision, planning, and inference systems.
robots.selection_reason: Included for market relevance and public recognition, while explicitly avoiding the unsupported Gen 2 or Gen 3 labels commonly used in secondary coverage.

market.availability: No public retail offer or confirmed external delivery schedule

specs.compute: Tesla AI, vision, planning, and inference stack; current robot hardware details not published on the official AI page
notes: No current-generation numeric specifications are entered because Tesla's official AI product page does not publish them.
Claims circulating in presentations or secondary media are intentionally excluded unless the manufacturer adds them to a stable current source.

store_price.basis: A deliberately fictional future-consumer tier price; it is not a Tesla quote, preorder price, forecast, or availability claim.

visual_identity.head: Featureless glossy black faceplate in a white helmet shell.
visual_identity.torso: Minimal white chest armor over a black waist and silver frame.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Human-proportioned white arms with black joints and slim five-finger hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: White thigh and shin panels with exposed black and silver mechanics.

blockers: Add current-revision full-body front, side, rear, hand, and material references; the official AI image does not identify a hardware generation.

open_issues: Current official specs and stable direct reference images are absent.
Optimus is not ready for 3D reference approval.

robots.summary: A home humanoid positioned around SwitchBot's Omni Sense VLA system for learning household workflows such as laundry and dish handling, shown publicly at CES 2026.

watch_video.title: onero H1 - Making embodied AI humanoid robots truly part of the home.

robots.selection_reason: Adds a directly consumer-positioned home humanoid with a visible store listing and aggressive sub-$10k headline price.

market.availability: Store page exists but product is marked unavailable

specs.compute: SwitchBot Omni Sense VLA; detailed onboard hardware not published
notes: The product page makes broad home-task claims but publishes no revision-specific numeric robot specifications.

public_price.notes: The current product page displays $9,999 while also marking the item unavailable; it is not treated as a completed purchasable market transaction.

store_price.basis: Current SwitchBot store page, with unavailable status retained.

visual_identity.head: Small smooth white head with a dark wraparound face area.
visual_identity.torso: Rounded white chest and abdomen with minimal consumer-electronics styling.
visual_identity.arms_hands: Slender white arms with black joints and anthropomorphic hands.
visual_identity.lower_body: Human-proportioned white legs with black joint bands and flat feet.

blockers: Add exact side, rear, hand, and material references.
Confirm final production design rather than CES preview design.

open_issues: No public physical specs.
Product is unavailable and may change before production.

research_summary.recommendation_basis: These four have strong official identity, current specs, useful full-body imagery, and limited revision ambiguity. All robots remain blocked from generation until explicit user approval.

validation.source_url_check: All source URLs returned HTTP 200 on 2026-07-18 except Tesla's official AI page, which returned HTTP 403 to automated curl while remaining the canonical manufacturer URL.
validation.threejs_or_mint_generation_check: threejs_app_started; Mint generation blocked because the current tool host exposes no Mint calls
